jokes.text: Futurama had never been cancelled by idiots, then brought back by bigger idiots.
jokes.setup: Hypnotist begins standard relaxation suggestion about feeling refreshed
jokes.punchline: References the show's real cancellation and revival, calling executives idiots
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke addressing the show's real-world cancellation/revival. Perfect economy and self-aware humor that only Futurama could pull off.

jokes.text: except no one drew magic-marker penises on my forehead
jokes.setup: Fry compares his memory loss to passing out in college
jokes.punchline: Specific detail about penis drawings that makes the comparison unexpectedly crude
jokes.explanation: Solid character-appropriate crude humor. The specificity of 'magic-marker penises' elevates it above generic college joke.

jokes.text: tender display of tonguesmanship
jokes.setup: Professor describes Fry and Leela's goodbye kiss
jokes.punchline: Made-up word 'tonguesmanship' that sounds academic but refers to kissing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor Farnsworth pseudo-scientific terminology for something crude. Great character voice and wordplay.

jokes.text: Sweet coincidence of Port-au-Prince, we're back at Earth!
jokes.setup: Characters realize they're back at Earth
jokes.punchline: Hermes' bizarre Jamaican-flavored exclamation combining 'sweet' with Port-au-Prince
jokes.explanation: Classic Hermes bureaucrat-meets-Jamaican voice. The Port-au-Prince reference is random but fits his character's Caribbean background.

jokes.text: Yes, it's sort of a Comedy Central Channel.
jokes.setup: Professor explains the Panama wormhole as Earth's central shipping channel
jokes.punchline: References Comedy Central, the network that aired Futurama
jokes.explanation: Another meta-reference to the show's network. Clean wordplay connecting 'central channel' to 'Comedy Central.'

jokes.text: We've lost power to the forward gameboy. Mario not responding!
jokes.setup: Spaceship losing power in emergency
jokes.punchline: Ship's systems are named after Nintendo games instead of technical terms
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of sci-fi tech speak. The juxtaposition of emergency situation with video game references works well.

jokes.text: Also mine has airconditioning.
jokes.setup: Everyone activating emergency safety spheres in crisis
jokes.punchline: Bender casually mentions his luxury feature during emergency
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - selfish and materialistic even in life-or-death moments. Great timing and character consistency.

jokes.text: I sublibed with only trivial braindabglid
jokes.setup: Professor explains he survived the crash with minor injuries
jokes.punchline: Made-up medical gibberish 'sublibed' and 'braindabglid' that sounds scientific
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor voice - complete medical nonsense delivered with scientific authority. The made-up words are phonetically convincing.

jokes.text: Right here behind this horrorcloth.
jokes.setup: Asked about the other crew members' condition
jokes.punchline: 'Horrorcloth' as a euphemism for what's covering badly injured bodies
jokes.explanation: 'Horrorcloth' is a brilliantly ominous made-up word that immediately conveys the severity of the situation with dark humor.

jokes.text: Move over God! Prepare for rebirth.
jokes.setup: Professor prepares to use stem cells to resurrect the crew
jokes.punchline: Casually telling God to move over, extreme hubris delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor megalomania. The casual dismissal of God while attempting to play God is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Wrong switch.
jokes.setup: Professor dramatically declares he'll play God and hits a switch
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits he hit the wrong switch, deflating the dramatic moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of dramatic tension. Two words that completely undercut the Professor's godlike proclamation.

jokes.text: Come on stem cells, work your astounding scientific nonsense.
jokes.setup: Professor attempts to use stem cells for resurrection
jokes.punchline: Calls his own science 'nonsense' while depending on it to work
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Professor acknowledges the absurdity of his own science while still believing in it.

jokes.text: Besides, these are adult stem cells, harvested from perfectly healthy adults whom I killed for their stem cells.
jokes.setup: Professor defends using stem cells by saying they're adult stem cells
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals he murdered people to get them
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that starts as ethical justification and becomes murder confession. The casual tone makes it darker and funnier.

jokes.text: O' man, I'm dripping with placenta. Good thing it's casual Friday.
jokes.setup: Hermes is reborn from stem cell treatment
jokes.punchline: Treats being covered in placenta as just a dress code issue
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hermes - reducing the miracle of rebirth to bureaucratic dress code concerns. The juxtaposition is absurdist gold.

jokes.text: Eh, nevermind.
jokes.setup: Hermes declares something will happen 'over my dead body'
jokes.punchline: Immediately backs down, deflating his own dramatic statement
jokes.explanation: Simple deflation joke. Hermes' cowardly nature undermining his tough talk is in character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I'm dying. I need mouth-to-ass resuscitation.
jokes.setup: Bender appears to be dying and needs help
jokes.punchline: Corrupts 'mouth-to-mouth' resuscitation to crude robot anatomy reference
jokes.explanation: Pure Bender - taking a medical emergency and making it crude. The wordplay on mouth-to-mouth is obvious but effective for the character.

jokes.text: I die happy knowing you fell for that.
jokes.setup: Fry tries to help dying Bender with mouth-to-ass resuscitation
jokes.punchline: Bender reveals it was a trick to get Fry to do something gross
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender manipulation. The misdirection works because it's exactly what Bender would do - con his friends even while dying.

jokes.text: That's because the doomsday device I showed all up in you puts out 15 gigawatts.
jokes.setup: Bender feels energetic after being revived
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions putting a doomsday device inside Bender for power
jokes.explanation: Typical Professor solution - use a doomsday device casually as a power source. The matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Who are you, my warranty?
jokes.setup: Professor warns about device exceeding Bender's limits
jokes.punchline: Bender treats safety concerns like warranty restrictions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender response - treats his robot nature literally while dismissing safety. Good mechanical metaphor.

jokes.text: If you stop partying for a single second, you'll explode and kill everyone here in a fireball melted gears and splattered bones!
jokes.setup: Professor explains Bender must burn off energy by partying
jokes.punchline: Graphically describes the consequences of stopping, making partying literally life-or-death
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that makes partying a matter of survival. The graphic imagery 'melted gears and splattered bones' is nicely specific.

jokes.text: Sounds like a party, baby! Oh yeah!
jokes.setup: Bender is told he'll explode and kill everyone if he stops partying
jokes.punchline: Treats mass death as just another party opportunity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender response - turning a threat into enthusiasm. His sociopathic joy at potential mass destruction is in character.

jokes.text: Hey, speaking of splattered bowels, can I cook you a romantic dinner tonight, Leela?
jokes.setup: Discussion of violent death and splattered organs
jokes.punchline: Fry uses this as a transition to ask Leela on a date
jokes.explanation: Brilliant non-sequitur showing Fry's obliviousness. Using 'splattered bowels' as a romantic dinner segue is perfectly wrong.

jokes.text: I'm poking as hard as I can!
jokes.setup: Fry trying to wake up comatose Leela with a stick
jokes.punchline: Innocent line that sounds sexual out of context
jokes.explanation: Juvenile double entendre. Works as intended but relies on cheap sexual innuendo rather than clever writing.

jokes.text: Because the pain slowly fades, but the love is forever.
jokes.setup: Fry asks why he should go on living
jokes.punchline: Professor gives unexpectedly wise, heartfelt advice
jokes.explanation: Surprising depth from Professor. The sincerity is funny because it's unexpected from this character.

jokes.text: Are you still hung-up on whatshername? Move on already!
jokes.setup: Fry is mourning Leela being in a coma
jokes.punchline: Bender dismisses Leela as 'whatshername' despite her being right there
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender callousness. Dismissing Leela as 'whatshername' when she's literally there in a coma is perfectly insensitive.

jokes.text: Maybe he's just trying to feed his family
jokes.setup: Discussion about the bathroom burglar at Planet Express
jokes.punchline: Fry sympathizes with someone stealing from bathrooms
jokes.explanation: Shows Fry's naive optimism - finding noble motivation for bathroom theft. The absurdity works with his character.

jokes.text: Analyzing... Analyzing.. Checking my e-bay bid... Dammit. Analysis complete.
jokes.setup: Computer analyzing Leela's personality from tapes
jokes.punchline: Gets distracted by eBay during important analysis
jokes.explanation: Humanizes the computer with relatable procrastination. The 'Dammit' suggests it lost the bid, adding character depth.

jokes.text: Hey, I was nuzling that.
jokes.setup: Computer extracting personality data
jokes.punchline: Fry was apparently being intimate with the robot during data transfer
jokes.explanation: Fry's inappropriate attachment to the robot. Shows his desperation and boundary issues.

jokes.text: I'm gonna hit the showers.
jokes.setup: Emotional moment between Fry and robot Leela
jokes.punchline: Bender awkwardly exits during intimate conversation
jokes.explanation: Bender's discomfort with emotions leads to abrupt exit. His timing makes it awkward.

jokes.text: What the.. Bad Nibbler, bad Nibbler!
jokes.setup: Robot Leela trying to hug Nibbler
jokes.punchline: Nibbler rejects her, sensing she's not real Leela
jokes.explanation: Animals sensing something wrong is a classic trope. Nibbler's rejection confirms the robot isn't convincing.

jokes.text: her opinion of gazelles - majestic. Her emotions - confused and hurt.
jokes.setup: Robot Leela listing what she inherited from real Leela
jokes.punchline: Treats random opinion about gazelles as important as complex emotions
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist touch - random gazelle opinion given equal weight to complex emotions. Shows how personality transfer might actually work.

jokes.text: I love you. I mean her. Wait, help me out here.
jokes.setup: Fry trying to express feelings to robot Leela
jokes.punchline: Gets confused about whether he loves her or the original
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry confusion. His inability to sort out complex emotions is very characteristic.

jokes.text: Really? No, don't get your hopes up
jokes.setup: Professor saying there's always hope
jokes.punchline: Immediately crushing that hope he just offered
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor cruelty - builds up hope just to tear it down immediately. Excellent structure and timing.

jokes.text: Frankly, she's just a brainless mound of scabs and puss.
jokes.setup: Professor says there's always hope for Leela, then corrects himself
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest medical description delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor bedside manner - crushingly honest medical assessment with no sensitivity. Dark but in character.

jokes.text: Can I? That's my speciality!
jokes.setup: Asked to declare patient legally dead
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg enthusiastically claims declaring death as his medical specialty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg - terrible at healing but enthusiastic about death. His pride in this 'specialty' is darkly funny.

jokes.text: her body is to be fed to the wild cyclophage, a highly endangered creature that eats only cyclopses
jokes.setup: Reading Leela's living will about what to do with her body
jokes.punchline: Creature that specifically eats one-eyed beings, playing on Leela's cyclops nature
jokes.explanation: Brilliant world-building joke. 'Cyclophage' is perfect wordplay and the specificity of eating only cyclopses is absurdist gold.

jokes.text: You think I wanna party? I'm sick of partying. Wohoo! More partying!
jokes.setup: Bender complaining about being forced to party constantly
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself with enthusiastic party cry
jokes.explanation: Shows Bender's internal conflict - forced to party against his will but compulsive nature takes over. Good character comedy.

jokes.text: And can you believe she wore a tanktop to my funeral?
jokes.setup: Robot Leela seeing real Leela at her funeral
jokes.punchline: Being offended by fashion choice at own funeral
jokes.explanation: Perfect vanity humor - even at her own resurrection, focusing on inappropriate fashion. Great character priority joke.

jokes.text: And can you believe she wore a tanktop to my funeral?
jokes.setup: Robot Leela sees real Leela awake at her own funeral
jokes.punchline: More concerned about inappropriate funeral attire than the resurrection
jokes.explanation: Perfect priority inversion - resurrection is less important than funeral fashion. Very Leela-like concern about propriety.

jokes.text: No, you shut up we got to get out of here.
jokes.setup: Robot Leela telling everyone to shut up and escape
jokes.punchline: Real Leela copies the line but corrects the grammar
jokes.explanation: Subtle character difference. Real Leela automatically corrects robot Leela's grammar even in crisis.

jokes.text: There would be if you'd taken a spin class instead of just lounging around in a coma!
jokes.setup: Real Leela says there's no room for two captains in the chair
jokes.punchline: Blames real Leela's weight gain from being in a coma
jokes.explanation: Brilliant victim-blaming - suggesting Leela should have exercised while in a coma. The absurdity is perfect for the character conflict.

jokes.text: I have to go. This is just too freaky.
jokes.setup: Both Leelas say the exact same thing
jokes.punchline: Identical response highlighting their similarity
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing how identical they are. The repetition emphasizes the uncanny nature of the duplication.

jokes.text: Please don't get upset, Leela. She's nothing like you. I have to go. This is just too freaky
jokes.setup: Fry trying to reassure robot Leela she's different
jokes.punchline: Robot Leela responding with exact same words as real Leela just did
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that proves Fry wrong immediately. Perfect timing and irony - robot is exactly like real Leela.

jokes.text: I hate partying! If only I didn't have so much cronk in my donkydonk.
jokes.setup: Bender explaining why he can't stop partying
jokes.punchline: Uses made-up robotic anatomical terms
jokes.explanation: Funny robot terminology. 'Cronk in my donkydonk' sounds appropriately mechanical and crude for Bender.

jokes.text: I hate partying! If only I didn't have so much cronk in my donkydonk.
jokes.setup: Bender lamenting his forced partying situation
jokes.punchline: Made-up robot anatomy terms that sound vaguely sexual
jokes.explanation: Good Bender voice with made-up robot terminology. 'Cronk' and 'donkydonk' sound appropriately mechanical and crude.

jokes.text: I'll kill you you slutclops!
jokes.setup: Robot Leela attacks real Leela in jealous rage
jokes.punchline: Combines 'slut' with 'cyclops' for cyclops-specific insult
jokes.explanation: Fantastic portmanteau insult specific to Futurama. 'Slutclops' is both crude and character-specific, perfect for the catfight.

jokes.text: But how do I know who's the human and who's the robot?
jokes.setup: Both Leelas tell Fry to shoot the other one
jokes.punchline: Fry genuinely can't tell them apart despite obvious differences
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry obliviousness. Standard 'can't tell who's real' trope but works for his character's density.

jokes.text: I'm a robot too!
jokes.setup: Fry shoots and reveals the identity twist
jokes.punchline: Bigger twist that Fry is also a robot
jokes.explanation: Great plot twist reveal. Escalates the robot revelation beyond just Leela to include Fry, genuinely surprising.

jokes.text: he just bubbled away like flem on a hot sidewalk
jokes.setup: Professor explains what happened to real Fry in the stem cells
jokes.punchline: Graphic simile comparing human dissolution to street phlegm
jokes.explanation: Vivid and disgusting imagery delivered with Professor's clinical detachment. The simile is memorably gross.

jokes.text: overstuffed him with copperwadding
jokes.setup: Professor explains Leela's mistake building robot Fry
jokes.punchline: Robot anatomy detail that sounds both technical and ridiculous
jokes.explanation: 'Copperwadding' sounds appropriately technical for robot construction while being absurd enough to be funny.

jokes.text: My God, I can't believe I'll never see that particular Fry again.
jokes.setup: Professor reflects on the death of the original Fry
jokes.punchline: Treats unique individual as just a 'particular' version
jokes.explanation: Brilliant understatement that reduces human uniqueness to product variation. Perfectly cold Professor perspective on death.

jokes.text: My God, I can't believe I'll never see that particular Fry again.
jokes.setup: Professor finishing explanation of original Fry's death
jokes.punchline: Shows rare emotion but specifies 'that particular' Fry like he's a model number
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment. Professor shows genuine grief but can't help treating Fry like a scientific specimen.

jokes.text: Whoa, cool! Hasta la vista, weener.
jokes.setup: Robot Fry and Leela decide to leave their human friends
jokes.punchline: Terminator reference combined with juvenile insult
jokes.explanation: Obvious Terminator reference but fits robot context. The 'weener' insult is juvenile but appropriate for Fry's character.

jokes.text: Why did their voices change?
jokes.setup: Robot versions speak in different robot voices when they leave
jokes.punchline: Character acknowledges unexplained voice change
jokes.explanation: Nice lampshade hanging on arbitrary voice change. Meta-humor acknowledging animation conventions.

jokes.text: That's the one thing we'll never truly understand.
jokes.setup: Leela asks why robot voices changed
jokes.punchline: Professor treats voice change as universe's greatest mystery
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up to voice change question. Makes minor plot hole into cosmic mystery, very Professor-like.

jokes.text: No chance high-pants!
jokes.setup: Told to party to save everyone's lives
jokes.punchline: Refuses with nonsensical insult 'high-pants'
jokes.explanation: 'High-pants' is a wonderfully random Bender insult. Fits his character's tendency toward non-sequitur put-downs.

jokes.text: No chance high-pants! I'd rather die and kill all of you than party for one more millisecond!
jokes.setup: Everyone telling Bender to keep partying to stay alive
jokes.punchline: Bender prefers death and mass murder to continuing party
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender extremism. His hatred of forced fun overrides self-preservation and concern for others.

jokes.text: Jeesh, what does it take to kill me?
jokes.setup: Bender survives massive explosion that should have killed everyone
jokes.punchline: Frustrated that he's hard to kill despite wanting to die
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender complaint - annoyed at his own indestructibility when he wants to stop existing. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Sweet mercy! My hellish nightmare is over! I never have to party again.
jokes.setup: Bender is cured of his doomsday device problem
jokes.punchline: Treats end of forced partying as religious deliverance
jokes.explanation: Great payoff to Bender's forced party arc. Religious language for end of partying is perfectly over-dramatic for Bender.
